zh.lifeSummary: Margeret de Bermingham於1340年出生在愛爾蘭庫迪 吉爾達卡布瑞 安德 登費斯。她於1353年與Sir Robert Preston 1st Baron of Gormaston在愛爾蘭萊音斯特迪 本 米亞斯結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1361年7月19日在她的家鄉享年21歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bermingham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Liam, Sinead.
Irish (of English origin): variant of   Birmingham  . This form of the surname is found mainly in Ireland.

History: The Bermingham family take their surname from lands held at what is now a great city in the West Midlands of England, but it was brought to Ireland as early as the 12th century. The Norman baron Robert de Bermyngeham was a follower of Strongbow (see Clare ); his name is found on a number of Irish charters between 1175 and 1179. The family was also known as Mac Fheorais (see Corish ) in Ireland, named for Piers (Gaelic Feoras) de Bermyngeham, the father of Robert.

variants.name: Birmingham
meanings: English: habitational name from Birmingham in the West Midlands. In Domesday Book the name is already found as Bermingeham, but it seems likely that it was originally Beornmundingahām ‘homestead (Old English hām) of the people of (-inga-) Beornmund’, a personal name composed of the elements beorn ‘young man, warrior’ + mund ‘protection’. This surname is established in Ireland (see Bermingham ).

variants.name: Corish
meanings: shortened form of Mac Fheorais ‘son of Piers’. The name was borne by a Connacht family descended from Piers de Bermingham, second lord of Athenry in Galway (died c. 1309). Compare Bermingham .
shortened form of Mac Muirghis ‘son of Maurice’, a personal name introduced to Ireland by the Normans. Compare Morrissey .

nameMeanings.name: Margeret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

en.lifeSummary: Margeret de Bermingham was born in 1340, in Carbury and Dunfierth, County Kildare, Ireland. She married Sir Robert Preston 1st Baron of Gormaston in 1353, in Diocese of Meath, Leinster, Ireland. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died on 19 July 1361, in her hometown, at the age of 21.

fr.lifeSummary: Margeret de Bermingham est née en 1340, à Carbury and Dunfierth, County Kildare, Irlande. Elle a épousé Sir Robert Preston 1st Baron of Gormaston en 1353, à Diocese of Meath, Leinster, Irlande. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 19 juillet 1361, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 21 ans.

ko.Carbury and Dunfierth, County Kildare, I: 아일랜드 카운티 킬더르 카버이 앤드 던피르스
ko.lifeSummary: Margeret de Bermingham(은)는 1340년 아일랜드 카운티 킬더르 카버이 앤드 던피르스에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1353년 아일랜드 레인스터 디오스스 프 미스에서 Sir Robert Preston 1st Baron of Gormaston(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1361년 7월 19일 그녀의 고향에서 21세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ela casou-se com Sir Robert Preston 1st Baron of Gormaston em 1353, em Diocese of Meath, Leinster, Irlanda. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. ru.Carbury and Dunfierth, County Kildare, I: карбури анд дунфиирт, коунти каилдар, Ирландия
ru.lifeSummary: Margeret de Bermingham родилась в 1340 г. в местности карбури анд дунфиирт, коунти каилдар, Ирландия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Robert Preston 1st Baron of Gormaston в 1353 г. в местности диосес оф мит, линстир, Ирландия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла 19 июля 1361 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 21 год.
